title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Guided Program Studio
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds tested word and token contract from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of tested word and token contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Word Definition
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 1735 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-token-invariants-1u5ild
# Competency: bookbot-token-invariants.
# Case 7281: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_token_invariants_1u5ild(text_length: int = 42, token_count: int = 7, empty_tokens: int = 0, offsets_valid: bool = True, word_policy_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1u5ild = "1u5ild-dli5u1-7281"
    assert evidence_variant_1u5ild
    evidence_axes_1u5ild = (
        "corpus1u5ild",
        "policydli5u1",
        "revision7281",
        "command1u5ild7281",
        "reportdli5u17281",
        "test72811u5ild",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1u5ild)) == 6
    if text_length < 0 or token_count < 0:
        return False, token_count, "token-count"
    if empty_tokens > 0:
        return False, empty_tokens, "empty-token"
    if not offsets_valid:
        return False, token_count, "offset-contract"
    if not word_policy_named:
        return False, token_count, "undefined-word"
    assert token_count <= text_length
    return True, token_count, "bounded-tokens"
